titleStmt.title: Jordan – Bani Saxar – Tribal History; Wars; Camels
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Jordan Fieldwork Campaign Antonella Torzullo 2022 OEAW

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: They say a ǧinn lived in the wādi so they escaped
in that time there was not funeral gathering
was there funeral gathering befor Abu ***?
wherever they where, they bury him
I mean, why did they leave?
no no, this is not correct
no, another thing I heard, they said, when the dead dies, they do not take it from the front of th tent, they take it from the back, is this right?
Fine, how about the poems about Bani Ṣaxar?
look, al-Mḥayḏ̣ar is in Wādi Sirḥān
if it is said to you, if it is said to you male camel, look it is from the camels
their names, their names, names of the she.-camels
they, their colors, it is not just one
no, no, the ṣafra is the normal, the ṣafr, the color is yellow
the yellow she camel... that does not...
they call it šagḥa, and... and that
now, the black from the maǧāhim, the malḥa is like the raven, this is malḥa, and there is ṣafra, that is ...
this is a relative, what is the name of your brother who stayed in ʕIrāg?
the green tatoo this is beauty, beauty
